step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the previous assessment. The anterior two-thirds of the tongue indeed receives primary innervation from the facial nerve via its chorda tympani branch, along with contributions from the lingual nerve from the mandibular division of the trigeminal nerve. This combination ensures proper sensory function including taste perception in this area.
raw_response:
I agree with the previous assessment. The anterior two-thirds of the tongue indeed receives primary innervation from the facial nerve via its chorda tympani branch, along with contributions from the lingual nerve from the mandibular division of the trigeminal nerve. This combination ensures proper sensory function including taste perception in this area.

Answer: A
